rationale: Across 32 ML/AI Platform Engineer postings observed between 2026-03-01 and 2026-06-01, the most material shift since the prior cycle is the GenAI platform mandate now landing at L30 ML Platform roles (not just at AI labs). Three previously-research-only surfaces are now explicit platform-team deliverables in postings at Reddit, Anthropic, DoorDash, Mixpanel, Glean, Fireworks, Together AI, OpenAI, Stripe, Red Hat, Samsara, Roblox, and Match Group: (1) LLM serving engines (vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, KServe/LLM-D) integrated as platform-managed runtimes with autoscaling and capacity planning; (2) LLM Gateways with model routing, token/rate limits, intelligent failover, and prompt/version management as a platform feature; (3) Evaluation harnesses (including LLM-as-judge) and agent observability as platform-owned surfaces with SDK integration into the shipping process. Current Modules 01-09 and Projects 01-05 fully cover the classical ML platform surfaces (feature stores, registries, workflow orchestration, multi-tenancy, DX, SDKs) — postings still cite those, but they are no longer the differentiator. The three proposed modules and one project address the gaps that are now table stakes. Ray-on-Kubernetes is a smaller, exercise-level gap; everything else with thin evidence (RAG infra, research data platform, edge ML) stays on the watch list per the contract.

modules.title: LLM Serving as a Platform Runtime
modules.description: Treat vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, and KServe (with LLM-D) as first-class platform-managed serving runtimes rather than tools the inference team installs. Covers what an ML Platform Engineer owns: choosing serving engines per workload, integrating them under a unified deployment abstraction, capacity planning under continuous-batching/KV-cache constraints, autoscaling LLM replicas, multi-region/multi-cloud routing, and exposing all of this via the platform's existing model registry and SDK. Kernel-level optimization is explicitly OUT of scope — that belongs to the Performance Engineer peer role.

exercises.title: Deploy vLLM as a multi-tenant serving runtime
exercises.summary: Stand up vLLM behind a KServe InferenceService with tenant-scoped quotas; expose via the platform's model deployment API.

exercises.title: Capacity planning under KV-cache constraints
exercises.summary: Given a workload mix (prompt-length distribution, concurrency target, p99 latency budget), size GPU pools and configure continuous batching.

exercises.title: Unified serving abstraction across engines
exercises.summary: Wrap vLLM, SGLang, and TensorRT-LLM behind a single platform deployment CRD that picks the engine based on model + workload class.

modules.rationale: Maps to `req-llm-serving-platform`. Current Module 06 covers classical model registries and deployments but says nothing about continuous batching, prefill/decode, KV-cache awareness, or how to expose LLM-specific serving runtimes through the platform's deployment surface. Ten postings explicitly require this knowledge AT the ML Platform Engineer level (not at Performance Engineer level).

evidence.title: Principal ML Engineer, Distributed vLLM Inference
evidence.quote: Tackle challenges in scalable inference systems and Kubernetes-native deployments via vLLM and LLM-D.

evidence.title: Senior ML Engineer, Voice AI
evidence.quote: Hands-on LLM serving engines: vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM.

evidence.title: Member of Technical Staff, Cloud Infrastructure
evidence.quote: Spearhead the creation of one of the world's first virtual clouds, seamlessly serving AI workloads across the globe.

evidence.title: Software Engineer, Cloud Inference
evidence.quote: End-to-end product of Claude on each cloud platform, from API integration and intelligent request routing to inference execution.

evidence.title: Software Engineer, AI Infrastructure
evidence.quote: Basic LLM knowledge: context length, prefill, KV cache.

evidence.title: Platform ML Engineering Manager, Inference
evidence.quote: Lead critical work on our shared internal inference stack.

evidence.title: Senior ML Engineer, AI Platform
evidence.quote: The AI Platform team supports hundreds of ML use cases and billions of inferences daily.

evidence.title: Staff ML Engineer - ML Infrastructure
evidence.quote: Computer vision and/or LLM production systems.

evidence.title: SWE, ML Infrastructure
evidence.quote: Build scalable, reliable services for notebooks, ML model training, experimentation, serving, and LLM applications.

exercise.title: KubeRay as a self-service compute primitive
exercise.summary: Stand up KubeRay on the platform cluster; expose Ray Jobs/Serve to platform users via the existing workflow SDK; cover RayCluster lifecycle, head/worker autoscaling, and integration with the platform's multi-tenancy primitives.

evidence.title: Senior ML Engineer, ML Training Platform
Staff Software Engineer, ML Platform
evidence.quote: Architect high-throughput distributed inference (Spark, Dask, Ray).
